page: Amazon Web Services c9g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It delivers a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 96.0 GB of system memory, which equates to exactly 2.0 GB of memory per core.

The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation with a single thread per core. This architecture eliminates resource contention associated with simultaneous multithreading. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom Arm-based Graviton architecture is designed to offer high compute density and power efficiency compared to traditional x86 architectures. The 1:2 resource ratio makes the c9g.12xlarge suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Recommended workloads include high-performance computing, batch processing, video encoding, gaming servers, and scientific modeling.
description: Amazon Web Services c9g.12xlarge is an arm64-based, compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, allocating 2.0 GB of memory per core. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides dedicated CPU resources with one thread per core, eliminating simultaneous multithreading overhead. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU acceleration, but it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Designed for qualitative cost efficiency through its custom Arm architecture, the c9g.12xlarge is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, media encoding, scientific modeling, and distributed analytics that require high CPU density without massive memory footprints.
